title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking: Streamlined Permitting Process for Critical Minerals Mining Projects on Federal Land
content: The Department of the Interior proposes a rule to consolidate environmental review timelines for critical minerals mining projects located on federal land, with the stated goal of reducing average permit approval time from 24 months to 12 months. The rule would apply to lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth element projects. Public comments are open for 60 days.

title: Final Rule: Updated List of Critical Minerals Adds Two New Elements
content: The final rule updates the official critical minerals list to include two additional elements used in battery and defense manufacturing. Companies producing or processing the newly listed minerals become eligible for expedited permitting and certain federal funding programs under the Defense Production Act.

title: Notice: Extension of Public Comment Period for Mining Royalty Fee Schedule
content: The comment period for the proposed hardrock mining royalty fee schedule has been extended by 30 days following requests from industry stakeholders. No substantive changes to the proposed fee structure have been made at this time.
